'''倶利伽羅'''（くりから）とはインドのサンスクリット語で、「剣に黒い龍の巻きついた不動尊」の意。 *[[倶利伽羅不動尊]] *[[倶利伽羅村]] *[[倶利伽羅駅]] *[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]] *[[倶利伽羅竜王]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]] *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] {{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}

'''倶利伽羅'''（くりから）とはインドのサンスクリット語で、クリカーラ（クリ＝黒、カーラ＝黒、時間の意味）。黒い竜王として知られる。「剣に黒い龍の巻きついた不動尊」と意訳される。 *[[倶利伽羅不動尊]] *[[倶利伽羅村]] *[[倶利伽羅駅]] *[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]] *[[倶利伽羅竜王]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]] *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] {{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}
'''倶利伽羅'''、'''倶梨迦羅'''（くりから） *[[倶利伽羅不動尊]] *[[倶利伽羅竜王]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]] *[[倶利伽羅村]] *[[倶利伽羅駅]] *[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]] *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] {{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}
'''倶利伽羅'''、'''倶梨迦羅'''（くりから） *[[倶利迦羅不動寺|倶利伽羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅竜王]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]] *[[倶利伽羅村]] *[[倶利伽羅駅]] *[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]] *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] {{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}
'''倶利伽羅'''、'''倶梨迦羅'''（くりから）は、[[石川県]][[津幡町]]に存在する地名。 *[[倶利迦羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅竜王]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]] *[[倶利伽羅村]] *[[倶利伽羅駅]] *[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]] *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] {{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}

'''倶利伽羅'''（くりから '''倶梨迦羅'''とも表記）は、[[サンスクリット]]「kulihah」に由来する[[不動明王]]の化身とされる[[竜]]（[[竜王]]）の名。剣（[[倶利伽羅剣]]）に絡みついた黒い竜の姿で表される。'''倶利伽羅竜王'''。
== 地名 == [[石川県]][[津幡町]]に存在する地名。旧[[倶利伽羅村]]。現在、[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]]がある。
*[[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）、[[元正天皇]]の勅願により[[善無畏]]が倶利迦羅不動明王像を奉安したと伝えられる[[倶利迦羅不動寺]]が建立される。 *近辺の[[加賀国]]と[[越中国]]の国境の[[峠]]が[[倶利伽羅峠]]と呼称され、[[1183年]]（[[寿永]]2年）に、[[源義仲]]軍と[[平維盛]]率いる平家軍との間で戦われた合戦[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]]がなされ、[[平家物語]]などにより、全国的に著名となる。 *[[1889年]]（[[明治]]22年）、 [[町村制]]施行により[[倶利伽羅村]]が成立。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）、北陸本線の駅として[[倶利伽羅駅]]が開設。 *[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）、津幡町に編入される。
== その他 == *[[倶利伽羅紋紋]] - [[入れ墨|刺青]]の[[デザイン|意匠]]のひとつ。その姿が江戸時代に好んで入れ墨の図案とされ、現代でもこれを[[博徒]]が好んで入れ、刺青の別称ともなっている。略されて「紋々」とも呼ばれる。 *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]]
{{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}} [[Category:不動明王]] [[Category:日本の地名]]

'''倶利伽羅'''（くりから '''倶梨迦羅'''とも表記）は、[[サンスクリット]]「kulihah」に由来する[[不動明王]]の化身とされる[[竜]]（[[竜王]]）の名。剣（[[倶利伽羅剣]]）に絡みついた黒い竜の姿で表される。'''倶利伽羅竜王'''。
== 地名 == [[石川県]][[津幡町]]に存在する地名。旧[[倶利伽羅村]]。現在、[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]]がある。
*[[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）、[[元正天皇]]の勅願により[[善無畏]]が倶利迦羅不動明王像を奉安したと伝えられる[[倶利迦羅不動寺]]が建立される。 *近辺の[[加賀国]]と[[越中国]]の国境の[[峠]]が[[倶利伽羅峠]]と呼称され、[[1183年]]（[[寿永]]2年）に、[[源義仲]]軍と[[平維盛]]率いる平家軍との間で戦われた合戦[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]]がなされ、[[平家物語]]などにより、全国的に著名となる。 *[[1889年]]（[[明治]]22年）、 [[町村制]]施行により[[倶利伽羅村]]が成立。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）、北陸本線の駅として[[倶利伽羅駅]]が開設。 *[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）、津幡町に編入される。
== その他 == *[[倶利伽羅紋紋]] - [[入れ墨|刺青]]の[[デザイン|意匠]]のひとつ。その姿が江戸時代に好んで入れ墨の図案とされ、現代でもこれを[[博徒]]が好んで入れ、刺青の別称ともなっている。略されて「紋々」とも呼ばれる。 *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] [[Category:不動明王]] [[Category:日本の地名]] *[[倶利迦羅不動寺|倶利伽羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]]
{{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}

== 関連項目 == *[[倶利伽羅紋紋]] - [[入れ墨|刺青]]の[[デザイン|意匠]]のひとつ。その姿が江戸時代に好んで入れ墨の図案とされ、現代でもこれを[[博徒]]が好んで入れ、刺青の別称ともなっている。略されて「紋々」とも呼ばれる。 *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] [[Category:不動明王]] [[Category:日本の地名]] *[[倶利迦羅不動寺|倶利伽羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]]
{{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}}

== 関連項目 == *[[日本号 (槍)|日本号]] *[[倶利伽羅紋紋]] - [[入れ墨|刺青]]の[[デザイン|意匠]]のひとつ。その姿が江戸時代に好んで入れ墨の図案とされ、現代でもこれを[[博徒]]が好んで入れ、刺青の別称ともなっている。略されて「紋々」とも呼ばれる。 *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利迦羅不動寺|倶利伽羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]]

revision.text:
'''倶利伽羅'''（くりから '''倶梨迦羅'''とも表記）は、[[サンスクリット]]「kulihah」に由来する[[不動明王]]の化身とされる[[竜]]（[[竜王]]）の名。剣（[[倶利伽羅剣]]）に絡みついた黒い竜の姿で表される。'''倶利伽羅竜王'''。
== 地名 == [[石川県]][[津幡町]]に存在する地名。旧[[倶利伽羅村]]。現在、[[道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷]]がある。
*[[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）、[[元正天皇]]の勅願により[[善無畏]]が倶利迦羅不動明王像を奉安したと伝えられる[[倶利迦羅不動寺]]が建立される。 *近辺の[[加賀国]]と[[越中国]]の国境の[[峠]]が[[倶利伽羅峠]]と呼称され、[[1183年]]（[[寿永]]2年）に、[[源義仲]]軍と[[平維盛]]率いる平家軍との間で戦われた合戦[[倶利伽羅峠の戦い]]がなされ、[[平家物語]]などにより、全国的に著名となる。 *[[1889年]]（[[明治]]22年）、 [[町村制]]施行により[[倶利伽羅村]]が成立。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）、北陸本線の駅として[[倶利伽羅駅]]が開設。 *[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）、津幡町に編入される。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本号 (槍)|日本号]] *[[倶利伽羅紋紋]] - [[入れ墨|刺青]]の[[デザイン|意匠]]のひとつ。その姿が江戸時代に好んで入れ墨の図案とされ、現代でもこれを[[博徒]]が好んで入れ、刺青の別称ともなっている。略されて「紋々」とも呼ばれる。 *[[倶利伽羅 (漫画家)]] *[[倶利伽羅峠]] *[[倶利迦羅不動寺|倶利伽羅不動寺]] *[[倶利伽羅剣]]
{{Aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりから}} [[Category:不動明王]] [[Category:日本の地名]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[倶利伽羅]] to [[Kurikara Dragon King]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倶利伽羅]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11387102]]
Wikidata name: Kurikara
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kurikara''' (倶利伽羅, also written 倶梨迦羅) is the name of a [[:en:Dragon King|dragon king]], considered a manifestation of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], derived from the Sanskrit term "kulihah." It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|qq=|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}. This image is called the '''Kurikara Dragon King'''.

==Place Name==
Kurikara is also a locality in [[:en:Tsubata, Ishikawa|Tsubata, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|qq=|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|qq=|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641153}}.

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk [[:en:Śubhakarasiṃha|Zenmui]] is said to have enshrined an image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}.
* The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|qq=|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]].
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

===See Also===
* {{ill|Nihongō (spear)|ja|日本号 (槍)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihongō (spear)|qq=|1=Nihon-gō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507520}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon."
* {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|qq=|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|qq=|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}

{{Disambiguation}}

{{translated page|ja|倶利伽羅|version=100207254|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon."
* {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Kurikara''' (倶利伽羅, also written 倶梨迦羅) is the name of a [[:en:Dragon King|dragon king]], considered a manifestation of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], derived from the Sanskrit term "kulihah." It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}. This image is called the '''Kurikara Dragon King'''.

==Place Name==
Kurikara is also a locality in [[:en:Tsubata, Ishikawa|Tsubata, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641153}}.

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk [[:en:Śubhakarasiṃha|Zenmui]] is said to have enshrined an image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}.
* The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]].
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

===See Also===
* {{ill|Nihongō (spear)|ja|日本号 (槍)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihongō (spear)|1=Nihon-gō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507520}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon."
* {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}

* The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]].
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

===See Also===

* {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon."

Wikidata name: Kurikara
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kurikara''' (倶利伽羅, also written 倶梨迦羅) is the name of a [[:en:Dragon King|dragon king]], considered a manifestation of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], derived from the Sanskrit term "kulihah." It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}. This image is called the '''Kurikara Dragon King'''.

==Place Name==
Kurikara is also a locality in [[:en:Tsubata, Ishikawa|Tsubata, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641153}}.

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk [[:en:Śubhakarasiṃha|Zenmui]] is said to have enshrined an image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}.
* The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]].
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

===See Also===

* {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon."
* {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk [[:en:Śubhakarasiṃha|Zenmui]] is said to have enshrined an image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}.
* The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]].
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}. * In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk [[:en:Śubhakarasiṃha|Zenmui]] is said to have enshrined an image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}. * The nearby mountain pass on the border of [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] and [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the [[:en:Battle of Kurikara Pass|Battle of Kurikara Pass]] took place here between the forces of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] and the Heike army led by [[:en:Taira no Koremori|Taira no Koremori]]. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system. * In 1909 (Meiji 42), [[:en:Kurikara Station|Kurikara Station]] opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line. * In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.
===See Also=== * {{ill|Nihongō (spear)|ja|日本号 (槍)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihongō (spear)|1=Nihon-gō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507520}} * {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon." * {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}} * {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}

==Place Name== Kurikara is also a locality in {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641153}}.

'''Kurikara''' (倶利伽羅, also written 倶梨迦羅) is the name of a {{ill|Dragon King|en|Dragon King|WD=Q135337914|lt=dragon king}}, considered a manifestation of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, derived from the Sanskrit term "kulihah." It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}. This image is called the '''Kurikara Dragon King'''.
==Place Name== Kurikara is also a locality in {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641153}}.
* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk {{ill|Śubhakarasiṃha|en|Śubhakarasiṃha|WD=Q1132525|lt=Zenmui}} is said to have enshrined an image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}. * The nearby mountain pass on the border of {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province}} and {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|WD=Q906899|lt=Etchū Province}} is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the {{ill|Battle of Kurikara Pass|en|Battle of Kurikara Pass|WD=Q2889028|lt=Battle of Kurikara Pass}} took place here between the forces of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} and the Heike army led by {{ill|Taira no Koremori|en|Taira no Koremori|WD=Q3055506|lt=Taira no Koremori}}. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}. * In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system. * In 1909 (Meiji 42), {{ill|Kurikara Station|en|Kurikara Station|WD=Q5360002|lt=Kurikara Station}} opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line. * In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.
===See Also=== * {{ill|Nihongō (spear)|ja|日本号 (槍)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihongō (spear)|1=Nihon-gō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507520}} * {{ill|Kurikara Tattoos|ja|倶利伽羅紋紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Tattoos|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A tattoo design featuring the Kurikara dragon motif, popular since the Edo period, especially among gamblers. It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon." * {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}} * {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk {{ill|Śubhakarasiṃha|en|Śubhakarasiṃha|WD=Q1132525|lt=Zenmui|1=Śubhakarasiṃha}} is said to have enshrined an image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}. * The nearby mountain pass on the border of {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} and {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|WD=Q906899|lt=Etchū Province|1=Etchū Province}} is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}}. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}. * In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system. * In 1909 (Meiji 42), {{ill|Kurikara Station|en|Kurikara Station|WD=Q5360002|lt=Kurikara Station|1=Kurikara Station}} opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line. * In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.

It has come to be a common alternate name for tattoos, sometimes abbreviated to "Monmon." * {{ill|Kurikara (manga artist)|ja|倶利伽羅 (漫画家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara (manga artist)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888140}} * {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387104}}

'''Kurikara''' (倶利伽羅, also written 倶梨迦羅) is the name of a {{ill|Dragon King|en|Dragon King|qid=Q135337914|lt=dragon king|1=Dragon King}}, considered a manifestation of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, derived from the Sanskrit term "kulihah." It is typically depicted as a black dragon entwined around a sword, known as the {{ill|Kurikara Sword|ja|倶利伽羅剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Sword|1=Kurikara sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387104}}. This image is called the '''Kurikara Dragon King'''.

==Place Name==
Kurikara is also a locality in {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata, Ishikawa|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture. It was formerly known as {{ill|Kurikara Village|ja|倶利伽羅村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Village|1=Kurikara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387103}}, and is now home to the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|ja|道の駅倶利伽羅 源平の郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Kurikara Genpei no Sato|1=Roadside Station Kurikara Genpei-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641153}}.

* In 718 (Yōrō 2), the monk {{ill|Śubhakarasiṃha|en|Śubhakarasiṃha|qid=Q1132525|lt=Zenmui|1=Śubhakarasiṃha}} is said to have enshrined an image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} at what became the {{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利伽羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利迦羅不動寺}}.
* The nearby mountain pass on the border of {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|qid=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} and {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|qid=Q906899|lt=Etchū Province|1=Etchū Province}} is known as {{ill|Kurikara Pass|ja|倶利伽羅峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Pass|1=Kurikara Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10888140}}. In 1183 (Juei 2), the {{ill|Battle of Kurikara Pass|en|Battle of Kurikara Pass|qid=Q2889028|lt=Battle of Kurikara Pass|1=Battle of Kurikara Pass}} took place here between the forces of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} and the Heike army led by {{ill|Taira no Koremori|en|Taira no Koremori|qid=Q3055506|lt=Taira no Koremori|1=Taira no Koremori}}. This battle is widely known due to accounts in works like the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|qid=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}.
* In 1889 (Meiji 22), Kurikara Village was formally established with the enforcement of the town and village system.
* In 1909 (Meiji 42), {{ill|Kurikara Station|en|Kurikara Station|qid=Q5360002|lt=Kurikara Station|1=Kurikara Station}} opened as part of the Hokuriku Main Line.
* In 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was incorporated into Tsubata Town.
